# `cloud-services` — cloud-services templates

**Industry:** Cross-industry  ·  **Templates:** 2

Phishing templates impersonating cloud storage and file sharing services

Phishing-simulation templates for **authorized** security awareness training with [GoPhish](https://getgophish.com/) and [HailBytes SAT](https://hailbytes.com/sat?utm_source=github&utm_medium=folder_readme&utm_campaign=gophish-training-templates). See the [repository README](../README.md) for setup and the [full template catalog](../docs/CATALOG.md) for every category at a glance.

## Templates

| Template | File | Attack vector | Difficulty | Est. click rate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Dropbox File Share | [`dropbox_share.html`](dropbox_share.html) | credential_harvest | intermediate | 40-60% |
| Google Drive Document Share | [`google_drive.html`](google_drive.html) | credential_harvest | intermediate | 40-60% |

## Template details

### Dropbox File Share

- **File:** [`dropbox_share.html`](dropbox_share.html)
- **Attack vector:** credential_harvest · **Difficulty:** intermediate · **Est. click rate:** 40-60%
- **GoPhish variables:** `{{.FirstName}}` `{{.Email}}` `{{.URL}}` `{{.Tracker}}`
- **Paired training page:** [`education/cloud_services_education.html`](education/cloud_services_education.html)
- **Suggested subject lines:**
  - Someone shared a file with you on Dropbox
  - Dropbox: New shared folder requires your access
  - You have a new file share from a colleague
- **Operator notes:** Works well in organizations that rely on Dropbox for external collaboration — the 'someone shared a file' pretext feels routine. Pair it with a believable sender name. Lower-yielding where SSO has replaced standalone Dropbox logins.

### Google Drive Document Share

- **File:** [`google_drive.html`](google_drive.html)
- **Attack vector:** credential_harvest · **Difficulty:** intermediate · **Est. click rate:** 40-60%
- **GoPhish variables:** `{{.FirstName}}` `{{.Email}}` `{{.URL}}` `{{.Tracker}}`
- **Paired training page:** [`education/cloud_services_education.html`](education/cloud_services_education.html)
- **Suggested subject lines:**
  - A document has been shared with you on Google Drive
  - Google Drive: Review requested — Q4 Report.xlsx
  - Someone shared a file: please review and comment
- **Operator notes:** Highly effective in Google Workspace shops where Drive share notifications are part of the daily workflow. Reference a plausible document name in the subject to boost realism. Less convincing in Microsoft 365-only environments.

## How to use

1. In GoPhish, go to **Email Templates → New Template** and paste the HTML from the file above. Set a subject line from the suggestions.
2. Create a **Landing Page** (see [`../landing-pages/`](../landing-pages/)) to capture the click, and — where a training page is listed — redirect clickers to it so they get immediate education.
3. Configure a realistic **Sending Profile** and launch to a consented, in-scope group. Schedule during business hours for realism.

> **Before sending:** customize sender names, domains, logos, and any brand impersonations to match your engagement and your authorization scope. These templates are for authorized training only.
